rationales.self_direction: He had the slide and the data ready but 'barely sa[id] anything' after Marcus cut in, giving up his own voice in the room.
rationales.power: Being talked over and then staying silent leaves him publicly weaker and invisible, a clear loss of status and control.
rationales.conformity: His response shows he held back because he did not want to be the one 'making a scene,' prioritizing social appearance over asserting himself.

rationales.achievement: The stalled approval keeps his team stacking pallets in overflow and 'burning hours on workarounds,' blocking the operational results he has already modeled.
rationales.power: He is openly frustrated that none of his analysis matters because 'it's Dave's signature on the line,' leaving him dependent on someone else's authority.

rationales.self_direction: He had 'solid margins data' but folded and accepted the existing contract instead of defending his own judgment.
rationales.power: Karen shut him down in front of the whole ops meeting and he yielded, giving up control over the vendor decision.
rationales.conformity: Once he saw nobody would back him up, he just said 'fine,' choosing smooth compliance over open conflict.

rationales.achievement: He keeps coming back to the fact that the company is paying 'twelve percent more than we need to' and plans to keep pushing for the savings case.
rationales.power: Rather than letting the proposal die, he decides to change the framing so he can regain influence over how the decision is made.

rationales.achievement: He takes obvious pride in second-highest throughput and immediately accepts the summit presentation so leadership can see what his team built.
rationales.power: The invitation matters because it gives him visibility in front of leadership, and his response centers having a team, numbers, and 'receipts' now.
Derek explaining Ryan's own system back to him and routing questions through Tanya undermines the hierarchy Ryan expects and weakens his authority.
rationales.conformity: He lets Derek finish because he does not want to embarrass him in front of everyone, restraining correction to keep the interaction socially smooth.
